mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.5%, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a random baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and residential mortgage debt indicate synchronized credit expansion and wealth effects that amplify aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across three consecutive rolling 5-year windows, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion when industrial production peaks, supply bottlenecks and capacity constraints compress profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns remain positive in over 55% of peak INDPRO observations, or the signal fails to predict earnings revision downturns within a 10-day window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate of 2.0 percent signals robust aggregate demand that lifts corporate revenues and earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward information coefficient drops below 0.02 over any 3-year period, or forward returns in high-GDP-growth quarters are not statistically distinct from low-growth quarters (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPIAUCSL index exceeding 0.25 percent triggers inflation hedging flows and anticipates Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of months where CPIAUCSL jumps above the 0.25% threshold, or the signal exhibits no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth exceeding 150,000 and a declining unemployment rate confirm labor market acceleration, which rapidly translates into higher consumer spending and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative Spearman rank correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across two consecutive 3-year rolling windows, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth accelerates above 3.0 percent annualized while industrial production simultaneously exceeds 105, demand outpaces supply capacity, triggering margin compression and valuation mean-reversion that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where both proxies exceed thresholds, or the joint signal fails to predict subsequent earnings downgrade cycles within a 3-month window.
